req.title: Inter-facility referral
req.statement: A clinician shall send a referral to another facility; the referral shall trigger a secure message and consent check.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-017 mandates three observable behaviours: (1) a clinician with a consenting patient can POST a ReferralRequest and receive 201 + stored resource + secure message dispatch; (2) a POST without patient consent must return 403; (3) a boundary case exercises the maximum-length referral note (4096 chars) to confirm the system stores it without truncation or error. EP covers the happy-path partition (valid clinician, consent present). NEG covers the error pathway (consent absent → 403). BVA covers the note-length boundary. STC is implicitly verified via state transition consent-present→referral-stored.

tests.name: test_referral_created_with_consent_and_secure_message_dispatched
preconditions: Authenticated clinician JWT available via <clinician_auth_headers> fixture
Patient P-001 has active consent record on file
Target facility F-002 exists in the system
Message broker spy available via <message_spy> fixture
actions: POST /ReferralRequest with valid payload referencing patient P-001 and facility F-002
Capture HTTP response status and Location header
Poll <message_spy> for dispatched secure message within timeout
expected: GET on returned Location returns the stored ReferralRequest
Exactly one secure message dispatched to facility F-002
trace: R-HLT-017: referral shall trigger a secure message
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_referral_created_with_consent_and_secure_message_dispatched(
    clinician_auth_headers, api_client, message_spy, ensure_consent, cleanup_referral
):
    ensure_consent(patient_id="P-001", granted=True)
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "ReferralRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/P-001"},
        "recipient": [{"reference": "Organization/F-002"}],
        "note": [{"text": "Routine cardiology follow-up"}]
    }

    resp = api_client.post(
        "/ReferralRequest",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    referral_id = body.get("id")
    assert referral_id, "Response must contain resource id"
    cleanup_referral(referral_id)

    fetch_resp = api_client.get(f"/ReferralRequest/{referral_id}", headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert fetch_resp.status_code == 200
    assert fetch_resp.json()["id"] == referral_id

    messages = message_spy.wait_for_messages(topic="secure-referral", count=1, timeout=5)
    assert len(messages) == 1
    assert messages[0]["recipient"] == "Organization/F-002"

tests.name: test_referral_rejected_403_when_consent_absent
preconditions: Authenticated clinician JWT available via <clinician_auth_headers> fixture
Patient P-002 has NO active consent record
Target facility F-002 exists in the system
actions: POST /ReferralRequest with valid payload referencing patient P-002 (no consent)
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-HLT-017: referral shall trigger a consent check
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_referral_rejected_403_when_consent_absent(
    clinician_auth_headers, api_client, message_spy, ensure_consent
):
    ensure_consent(patient_id="P-002", granted=False)
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "ReferralRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/P-002"},
        "recipient": [{"reference": "Organization/F-002"}],
        "note": [{"text": "Cardiology consult"}]
    }

    resp = api_client.post(
        "/ReferralRequest",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "consent" in body.get("issue", [{}])[0].get("diagnostics", "").lower() \
        or "consent" in str(body).lower(), "Response must reference consent denial"

    messages = message_spy.get_messages(topic="secure-referral", timeout=1)
    assert len(messages) == 0, "No secure message should be dispatched on 403"

tests.name: test_referral_note_at_maximum_length_boundary_stored_intact
preconditions: Authenticated clinician JWT available via <clinician_auth_headers> fixture
Patient P-001 has active consent record on file
Target facility F-002 exists in the system
System documented maximum note length is 4096 characters
actions: Construct referral note string of exactly 4096 characters
Retrieve the stored resource and compare note text length
expected: Stored note text length equals exactly 4096 characters without truncation
trace: BVA: maximum field length must be accepted and persisted verbatim
tests.executable:
import pytest

MAX_NOTE_LEN = 4096

def test_referral_note_at_maximum_length_boundary_stored_intact(
    clinician_auth_headers, api_client, ensure_consent, cleanup_referral
):
    ensure_consent(patient_id="P-001", granted=True)
    max_note = "A" * MAX_NOTE_LEN
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "ReferralRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/P-001"},
        "recipient": [{"reference": "Organization/F-002"}],
        "note": [{"text": max_note}]
    }

    resp = api_client.post(
        "/ReferralRequest",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201 at boundary, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    referral_id = resp.json().get("id")
    assert referral_id
    cleanup_referral(referral_id)

    fetch_resp = api_client.get(f"/ReferralRequest/{referral_id}", headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert fetch_resp.status_code == 200
    stored_note = fetch_resp.json()["note"][0]["text"]
    assert len(stored_note) == MAX_NOTE_LEN, (
        f"Note truncated: expected {MAX_NOTE_LEN}, got {len(stored_note)}"
    )

tests.name: test_referral_state_transition_active_to_completed
preconditions: Authenticated clinician JWT available via <clinician_auth_headers> fixture
Patient P-001 has active consent record
An existing ReferralRequest in status=active exists for patient P-001 (id from <active_referral> fixture)
actions: PUT /ReferralRequest/{id} with status changed to completed
Retrieve the resource and inspect status field
expected: State transition from active to completed accepted without error
trace: R-HLT-017: referral stored (lifecycle state must be persisted)
STC: active → completed transition must succeed
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_referral_state_transition_active_to_completed(
    clinician_auth_headers, api_client, active_referral, ensure_consent
):
    ensure_consent(patient_id="P-001", granted=True)
    referral_id = active_referral(patient_id="P-001", facility_id="F-002")

    fetch_resp = api_client.get(f"/ReferralRequest/{referral_id}", headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert fetch_resp.status_code == 200
    resource = fetch_resp.json()
    assert resource["status"] == "active", "Precondition: referral must start as active"

    resource["status"] = "completed"
    update_resp = api_client.put(
        f"/ReferralRequest/{referral_id}",
        json=resource,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )

    assert update_resp.status_code == 200, (
        f"Expected 200 on state transition, got {update_resp.status_code}: {update_resp.text}"
    )
    verify_resp = api_client.get(f"/ReferralRequest/{referral_id}", headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert verify_resp.json()["status"] == "completed"

tests.name: test_referral_decision_table_consent_and_role_combinations
preconditions: Patient P-001 has active consent record
Patient P-002 has NO consent record
actions: Attempt POST /ReferralRequest as nurse with consenting patient → expect 403
Attempt POST /ReferralRequest as clinician with non-consenting patient → expect 403
Confirm neither attempt creates a resource
expected: Non-clinician role with consent present returns 403
Clinician role with consent absent returns 403
No ReferralRequest resources persisted for either case
trace: DT: role × consent combinations that must all fail
